name: Low-dose klotho improved memory in aged rhesus macaques
summary: A single low-dose systemic klotho treatment improved spatial memory performance in aged rhesus macaques, while higher explored doses did not show the same cognitive improvement.
statement: In aged rhesus macaques, a single low-dose subcutaneous rhesus klotho treatment improved high-load and normal-load spatial delayed-response memory performance, with effects reported up to about two weeks after treatment.
population_or_model: Aged rhesus macaques with spatial delayed-response cognitive testing
time_horizon: Acute testing at 4 hours and repeated testing over about 1 to 2 weeks after a single dose
quantitative_note: The primary cognitive dose was 10 micrograms/kg; the study reported significant high-memory-load and normal-memory-load improvements, while 20 and 30 micrograms/kg did not show significant improvement.
caveats: This is nonhuman-primate evidence, not human efficacy.
The study used one cognitive task family and a limited number of treated animals.
The dose response was nonmonotonic, so therapeutic-window and safety interpretation remain early.
Cognitive improvement after one dose does not establish durable aging modification or LEV-relevant benefit.
